title: 〜させられる (Causative-Passive)
description: 〜させられる combines the causative (make/let) and passive (be done to) forms to mean you were forced to do something — usually against your will, with a nuance of reluctance or inconvenience.

content.text: The causative-passive is the "double-whammy": you take the causative form (〜させる, to make someone do) and put it into the passive (〜られる, to be on the receiving end). The result is 〜させられる — "to be made to do." It almost always carries a feeling of reluctance: you didn't want to, but you had no choice.

content.japanese: causative 〜させる ＋ passive 〜られる ＝ 〜させられる
上司（じょうし）に残業（ざんぎょう）させられた。
content.english: I was made to work overtime by my boss.

content.japanese: 子供（こども）の頃（ころ）、ピアノを習（なら）わされた。
content.english: When I was a kid, I was forced to take piano lessons.

content.japanese: 母（はは）に嫌（きら）いな野菜（やさい）を食（た）べさせられた。
content.english: My mom made me eat vegetables I hate.

content.japanese: 飲（の）み会（かい）で先輩（せんぱい）にたくさんお酒（さけ）を飲（の）まされた。
content.english: At the drinking party, my senpai made me drink a lot.

content.text: For Ichidan (ru-verbs), drop る and add させられる: 食（た）べる → 食（た）べさせられる. For Godan (u-verbs), shift the final kana to its あ-row sound and add される or せられる. The irregulars are する → させられる and くる → 来（こ）させられる.

rows: 飲（の）まされる ／ 飲（の）ませられる
書（か）かされる ／ 書（か）かせられる
話（はな）させられる（no contraction）

content.text: Godan verbs have a shortened form: 〜せられる contracts to 〜される, so 飲（の）ませられる becomes 飲（の）まされる. Both are correct, and the contracted form sounds more natural in speech. One exception: verbs ending in す (like 話（はな）す, 押（お）す) do NOT contract — you always say 話（はな）させられる, never ×話（はな）さされる, because the double さ sound is awkward (and 〜さされる collides with "to be stabbed").

content.japanese: Godan 〜ませられる → 〜まされる（す-ending verbs excluded）
カラオケで無理（むり）やり歌（うた）わされた。
content.english: I was forced to sing at karaoke.

content.japanese: 毎日（まいにち）漢字（かんじ）を百回（ひゃっかい）書（か）かされた。
content.english: I was made to write kanji a hundred times every day.

examples.japanese: causative 〜させる ＋ passive 〜られる ＝ 〜させられる
上司（じょうし）に残業（ざんぎょう）させられた。
examples.english: I was made to work overtime by my boss.

examples.japanese: 子供（こども）の頃（ころ）、ピアノを習（なら）わされた。
examples.english: When I was a kid, I was forced to take piano lessons.

examples.english: My mom made me eat vegetables I hate.

examples.english: At the drinking party, my senpai made me drink a lot.

examples.japanese: Godan 〜ませられる → 〜まされる（す-ending verbs excluded）
カラオケで無理（むり）やり歌（うた）わされた。
examples.english: I was forced to sing at karaoke.

examples.japanese: 毎日（まいにち）漢字（かんじ）を百回（ひゃっかい）書（か）かされた。
examples.english: I was made to write kanji a hundred times every day.
